text: A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was traveling northbound on an access road towards in Las Vegas, Nevada, when the driver disengaged for a vehicle in the oncoming lane rounding a tight curve. Approximately eleven seconds after disengaging, the driver proceeded in manual mode making a right turn and struck the curb at low speed. There were no injuries and police were not called.

text:
On November , 2024 at 12:01 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving an SUV on at . 
The Waymo AV was traveling north on behind an SUV. The SUV stopped within the lane of travel and the Waymo AV came to a stop behind it. The SUV then began reversing and the rear of the SUV made contact with the front of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On January , 2026 at 2:46 PM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Tempe, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger car on near .

The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on in the leftmost lane when a passenger car that was stopped facing north in a queue of traffic in the adjacent right lane, began to change lanes to the left and entered the Waymo AV's lane of travel, and the front left side of the passenger car made contact with the right side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On October , 2025 at 9:15 AM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Phoenix, Arizona was in a collision involving a trash can on near Exit . 
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on in the rightmost lane. A pickup truck that was traveling northbound on in the third from the right lane made contact with a trash can that was in the roadway. The trash can deflected off the pickup truck and rolled across the roadway. As the trash can was entering the Waymo AV's lane of travel, the test driver transitioned the Waymo AV to manual mode and began to maneuver to the right and the trash can made contact with the front of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was not engaged and a test driver was operating the Waymo AV in manual mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On January , 2026 at 3:53 AM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Tempe, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger car on at . 

The Waymo AV was stopped facing west in the rightmost lane of in a queue of traffic for a red light at the intersection with . A passenger car, traveling eastbound in the leftmost westbound travel lane at a high rate of speed, entered the intersection against a red light, and made contact with the front left of a second passenger car traveling northbound on through a green light. As a result of this contact, the airbags in both passenger cars deployed. The initial eastbound passenger car continued to exit the intersection after contact, proceeded towards the rightmost westbound lane on , and the front left of the passenger car made contact with the left side of the stationary Waymo AV, resulting in airbag deployment in the Waymo AV. After making contact with the Waymo AV, the passenger car continued eastbound and the left side of the passenger car then made contact with an additional Waymo AV (VIN ) that was stopped behind the initial Waymo AV in traffic. At the time of the impact, both Waymo AVs' Level 4 ADS were engaged in autonomous mode. The two passenger cars and the initial Waymo AV (VIN ) sustained damage. An occupant of the northbound passenger car was transported from the scene for medical treatment with unknown injuries.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
Waymo is submitting this updated report to correct an erroneously reported VIN for the Waymo AV. This updated report is being submitted the same day the original report was submitted, on the monthly reporting due date of October 15, 2024. The remainder of the report and narrative is unchanged.
On September, 2024 at 6:52 AM CT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in Austin, Texas was in a collision involving a heavy trucks trailer near and .
The Waymo AV was in the driveway of a business park in the inner lane near the curb preparing to turn right onto with a heavy truck in the left lane next to the Waymo AV, preparing to also turn right onto . The Waymo AV was stationary while yielding to the heavy truck and trailer making a right turn from the outer left lane when the trailers rear passenger side and rear tires made contact with the driver side front of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On January , 2025 at 3:32 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a cyclist on at .
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on in the rightmost lane, and came to a stop for a red light at the intersection with . While the Waymo AV remained stopped at the intersection, a cyclist traveling southbound against traffic proceeded through the intersection, passing the Waymo AV on the left, and the cyclist made contact with the driver side of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vulnerable road user was involved. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On April , 2025 at 7:44 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a heavy truck on near . 
The Waymo AV was stopped facing southeast on , yielding to a heavy truck traveling northwest in a narrow roadway with parked vehicles on both sides of the street. As the heavy truck passed the stopped Waymo AV, the driver side of the heavy truck cab made contact with the rear driver side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text:
On July , 2024 at 8:43 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in , California was in a collision involving a passenger car on . 
The Waymo AV was traveling east on . As the AV approached the all-way stop sign at , it detected a vehicle traveling west on and crossing into the opposing lane (the lane in which the AV was traveling). The passenger car entered the intersection in the opposing lane without slowing. The Waymo AV slowed down and maneuvered towards the right side of the eastbound lane as the front of the passenger car, still moving in the wrong direction in the eastbound lane, made contact with the front driver side corner of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away and because of airbag deployment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.
